mcqs.question: What is the primary goal of unit testing?

options.text: To verify that individual units of code work as expected
To verify that multiple units of code work together
To verify that the software meets the requirements
To verify that the software is reliable

mcqs.explanation: Unit testing is used to verify that individual units of code, such as functions or methods, work as expected.

mcqs.question: What is integration testing?

options.text: Testing individual units of code
Testing multiple units of code together
Testing the software as a whole
Testing the requirements

mcqs.explanation: Integration testing is the process of testing multiple units of code together to ensure they interact correctly.

mcqs.question: What is validation testing?

options.text: Testing individual units of code
Testing multiple units of code together
Testing the software meets the requirements
Testing the software is reliable

mcqs.explanation: Validation testing is the process of checking that the software meets the requirements and specifications.

mcqs.question: Why is testing important in software development?

options.text: To ensure the software is reliable
To ensure the software meets the requirements
To identify and fix defects early
To reduce costs

mcqs.explanation: Testing helps identify and fix defects early in the development process, ensuring the software is reliable and performs as expected.

mcqs.question: What is a test case?

options.text: A set of inputs, expected results, and execution conditions
A piece of code used to test a unit of code
A test suite
A testing framework

mcqs.explanation: A test case is a set of inputs, expected results, and execution conditions used to test a piece of software.

mcqs.question: What is a test suite?

options.text: A set of test cases
A piece of code used to test a unit of code
A testing framework
A test case

mcqs.explanation: A test suite is a collection of test cases used to test a piece of software.

mcqs.question: What is black box testing?

options.text: Testing with knowledge of the internal workings
Testing without knowledge of the internal workings
Testing with some knowledge of the internal workings
Testing the requirements

mcqs.explanation: Black box testing is a testing technique where the tester has no knowledge of the internal workings of the software.

mcqs.question: What is white box testing?

options.text: Testing without knowledge of the internal workings
Testing with knowledge of the internal workings
Testing with some knowledge of the internal workings
Testing the requirements

mcqs.explanation: White box testing is a testing technique where the tester has knowledge of the internal workings of the software.

mcqs.question: What is gray box testing?

options.text: Testing without knowledge of the internal workings
Testing with knowledge of the internal workings
Testing with some knowledge of the internal workings
Testing the requirements

mcqs.explanation: Gray box testing is a testing technique where the tester has some knowledge of the internal workings of the software.

mcqs.question: What is a testing framework?

options.text: A set of tools and libraries used to write and run tests
A piece of code used to test a unit of code
A test suite
A test case

mcqs.explanation: A testing framework is a set of tools and libraries used to write and run tests.

mcqs.question: What is test-driven development (TDD)?

options.text: Writing tests before writing code
Writing code before writing tests
Writing tests and code simultaneously
Not writing tests at all

mcqs.explanation: TDD is a software development process where tests are written before the code.

mcqs.question: What is behavior-driven development (BDD)?

options.text: Writing tests in a natural language style
Writing tests in a programming language
Writing tests before writing code
Not writing tests at all

mcqs.explanation: BDD is a software development process where tests are written in a natural language style.

mcqs.question: What is a mock object?

options.text: A fake object used in testing to isolate dependencies
A real object used in testing
A test case
A test suite

mcqs.explanation: A mock object is a fake object used in testing to isolate dependencies.

mcqs.question: What is a stub?

options.text: A small piece of code used in testing to replace a dependency
A large piece of code used in testing
A test case
A test suite

mcqs.explanation: A stub is a small piece of code used in testing to replace a dependency.

mcqs.question: What is a test double?

options.text: A fake object used in testing to replace a dependency
A real object used in testing
A test case
A test suite

mcqs.explanation: A test double is a fake object used in testing to replace a dependency.
